content: Lithium is the key input for lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles and grid storage. Battery-grade lithium carbonate equivalent (LCE) demand correlates directly with EV production. A typical 60 kWh EV battery requires approximately 8-10 kg of lithium. Global lithium demand is projected to grow 40x by 2040 under net-zero scenarios, making it the fastest-growing commodity by percentage.
